Becoming a Kubestronaut is an exciting opportunity for me to learn, collaborate, and share knowledge with the community.

person.name: Sanjay Nithish K S
person.bio: I am a Senior DevOps Engineer with 3+ years of experience designing and operating secure, scalable cloud platforms across AWS and Azure. My core expertise includes Infrastructure as Code using Terraform and Bicep, CI/CD automation with GitHub Actions and Azure DevOps, and implementing DevSecOps and FinOps best practices. I have led initiatives around landing zones, policy-driven governance, container security, and Kubernetes-based workloads in regulated environments. I am passionate about building reliable, production-grade cloud-native systems.

person.name: Giju Kang
person.bio: I am a software engineer with broad interests spanning development and infrastructure. I enjoy building products and exploring the diverse ecosystem of CNCF projects. Currently, I serve as a founding member of the CNCK (Cloud Native Community Korea) organizing team, where I contribute to growing the cloud-native community in Korea.

person.name: Richard Steinbiss
person.bio: Richard brings over seven years of consulting experience from Umlaut (part of Accenture) and Detecon (part of Deutsche Telekom), where he leveraged his data analytics and engineering skills to assist international clients in telecommunications and other industries. He holds a Master's degree with honors in Information and Communications Technology from RWTH Aachen University in Germany. Fully certified in Azure, AWS, and of course Kubernetes, Richard manages the data analytics and machine learning initiatives at OnRamp, a global fintech startup.

person.name: Tal Yitzhak
person.bio: I'm a passionate DevOps Engineer and Solutions Architect with expertise in cloud-native technologies and a strong focus on CNCF projects like ArgoCD, Argo Rollouts, OpenTelemetry, and Jaeger. Through my blog posts on Medium, active participation in the CNCF community, and contributions to various projects such as ArgoCD and Argo Rollouts, I aim to promote and educate others about the benefits of cloud-native technologies. As a CNCF member, I continue to support the community through my work with CNCF Projects i'm passionate about like Argo, OpenTelemetry and Jaeger. Connect with me on Medium (https://medium.com/@talyitzhak) to explore real-world production use cases and insights into CNCF technologies.

person.name: Venkata Satya Sameer Salapu
person.bio: I am a Cloud Deployment and Support Engineer at Ericsson, specializing in troubleshooting and resolving complex issues in cloud infrastructure and Kubernetes clusters running on bare metal for global telecom customers. My role involves helping organizations deploy, upgrade, and maintain Kubernetes environments on bare metal platforms, ensuring high availability and performance. I have a keen interest in system administration, automation, and the cloud-native ecosystem.

person.name: Jurgen Bruggeman

Currently I like spending my time on Kubernetes and help people/customers adopting the Cloud Native thought, preferably using my current employers own Kubernetes as a Service platform (PKE).

person.name: Koray Oksay
person.bio: He works at Kubermatic as a Kubernetes Consultant and Trainer to help companies with their cloud-native journey. Before that, he worked for startup and enterprise companies in the advertising, banking, and telecom industries as SysAdmin, Application Admin, DevOps Engineer, and SRE for more than 20 years.

person.name: Tommy Johannessen
person.bio: Dev at ❤️ - Ops by 🛠️  Currently helping customers with their cloud projects. Lifelong user and fan of open source technologies. Love to learn and master good tech!

person.name: Ahmed Mounani

person.name: Benson Lam

person.name: Marco Nilo
person.bio: I’m a cybersecurity enthusiast, especially when it comes to Kubernetes and containerized environments. I like focusing on Kubernetes security, from protecting clusters to container runtime security. I enjoy learning about attack patterns, vulnerabilities, best practices, and tools to make cloud-native environments more secure.

person.name: Than Htike Phyo
person.bio: I am a Network Security professional specializing in secure enterprise and hybrid infrastructure design, with strong expertise in next-generation firewalls, SD-WAN, SASE, and Zero Trust architectures. I work extensively with cloud-native and Kubernetes environments, focusing on CNCF technologies such as Kubernetes, service mesh, and cloud security integration. My experience spans secure campus networking, micro-segmentation, and integrating security into DevOps and container platforms. I am passionate about bridging traditional network security with modern cloud-native architectures. I regularly share insights and technical deep dives on infrastructure security and Kubernetes security best practices.

person.name: Jeonghoon Han

person.name: Aleksandr Varlamov
person.bio: With 20 years of experience in IT, telecom, and consulting, Aleksandr specializes in designing and delivering complex solutions and services at scale. He provides management and technical leadership to engineering and development teams, with a focus on Kubernetes, Cloud, and Security. His expertise also includes DevOps, GitOps, and DevSecOps.

person.name: Jorge O. Castro
person.bio: Jorge (@castrojo) is a Developer Relations specialist at the CNCF, with a focus on improving developer experience and communities. Jorge graduated with a degree in Telecommunications from Michigan State University and rode with the 11th Armored Cavalry Regiment for four years. He has contributed to Kubernetes, Ubuntu, Kubeflow, and the Cloud Custodian open source projects. He now resides in Ann Arbor, Michigan with his lovely wife Jill, their son Rafael, and their beagle Oscar. Hobbies and interests include heavy metal, passport renewal, paleontology, gaming, technology, and scouting out the best beers in the world.
